stories.title: Vertu wants executives to pay $6,880 for an AI agent — here’s how it actually performs
stories.simple_summary: A luxury phone company called Vertu is selling an AI assistant service for $6,880 per year. A tech reviewer tested whether it actually works well.
stories.why_it_matters: Unless you're shopping for very expensive luxury gadgets, this doesn't affect you — it's a niche product for wealthy people.

stories.title: Databricks hits $188B valuation, extending its run as AI’s favorite second act
stories.simple_summary: A company called Databricks became more valuable ($188 billion) by focusing on AI technology and showing that certain types of AI can save money on coding work.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies — nothing you need to act on, though it shows AI for coding is becoming mainstream.

stories.title: What to know about the AI chip stock selloff - ABC News
stories.source: Breaking News, Latest News and Videos
stories.simple_summary: The headline doesn't give enough details, but it appears to be about stock prices falling for companies that make AI computer chips.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly a stock market story — only relevant if you own these stocks or invest in tech.

stories.title: Dell Technologies vs. NVIDIA: Which Artificial Intelligence Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
stories.simple_summary: This is comparing whether to buy NVIDIA or Dell as stock investments based on their AI businesses.
stories.why_it_matters: This is only for investors considering which AI-related stocks to buy — not relevant for small business owners or everyday use.

stories.title: The Zoom hack that says, ‘Don’t record me’
stories.simple_summary: Zoom is now recording and summarizing meetings with AI, which raises privacy concerns — some people worry too many conversations are being automatically recorded and that no one will actually read all the summaries.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Zoom meetings for your business, be aware that conversations may be recorded and summarized by AI — you should check your privacy settings.

stories.title: Agility Robotics plants its flag in Tesla’s backyard
stories.simple_summary: A robotics company called Agility is opening a new training center near where Tesla is located to teach its robot called Digit.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly news for the robotics and tech industry — nothing immediate for small business owners, though it shows robot technology is advancing.

stories.title: AI-driven memory crunch jolts India’s smartphone market
stories.simple_summary: In India, smartphone prices and demand are changing because phone makers are adding more expensive AI features, causing a slowdown in how many phones people are buying.
stories.why_it_matters: If you buy budget smartphones, prices may rise as companies add AI features — but this is mainly affecting the Indian market right now.

stories.title: China’s Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi Model, Threatening America’s Lead
stories.simple_summary: China released a new AI model called Kimi that is competitive with American AI technology.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly tech industry news — it matters more to companies building AI rather than small business owners or everyday users.

stories.title: TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
stories.simple_summary: TikTok is testing a tool that can spot when someone uses AI to create a fake version of your face or voice, and lets you report it.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're a content creator on TikTok, this tool could help protect you from people making fake AI copies of you.

stories.title: Exclusive | SpaceX in Talks to Provide Computing Power for Pentagon’s AI Push
stories.simple_summary: SpaceX is talking with the Pentagon about providing computer power to help the military with AI projects.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a military and government contract story — it doesn't affect everyday business owners or regular people.

stories.title: MLB restricts using dugout iPads for AI-assisted in-game strategy
stories.simple_summary: Major League Baseball is now restricting teams from using iPads with AI tools to help make in-game strategy decisions during games.
stories.why_it_matters: This is only about professional baseball rules — it doesn't affect ordinary people or small businesses.

stories.title: How Apple’s big lawsuit could disrupt OpenAI’s IPO plans
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, claiming it stole trade secrets and hired many former Apple employees. This legal fight comes at a bad time as OpenAI is planning to go public (sell stock to the public).
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly important to investors and tech industry insiders — if you use ChatGPT, this doesn't change your experience, though it shows big tech companies are fighting over AI.
